Title: Melatonin Enhances Panax vietnamensis Resistance to Leaf Blight Pathogen Neofusicoccum ribis via the PvWRKY40–PvCOMT2 Module-Driven Lignin Biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies lignin monomer biosynthesis and promotes target-product accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes O-methylation of lignin pathway hydroxycinnamyl intermediates in the COMT family step contributing to monolignol formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Sustained upregulation during Neofusicoccum ribis infection; strongly induced by melatonin treatment and in MT-treated calli
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in infected and MT-treated Panax vietnamensis leaves/calli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; calli; stem/xylem in transgenic Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall reinforcement and disease resistance against fungal pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression significantly increases lignin content and enhances resistance to Fusarium oxysporum; MT-induced expression is associated with improved resistance to Neofusicoccum ribis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; transient overexpression in Panax vietnamensis calli; transient and stable overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; lignin quantification; pathogen resistance assay; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Neofusicoccum ribis infection; exogenous melatonin treatment; Fusarium oxysporum inoculation in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvCOMT2 was persistently induced during N. ribis infection and strongly activated by melatonin. 2. PvWRKY40 directly bound the PvCOMT2 promoter W-box at -491 bp and repressed its transcription. 3. Overexpression of PvCOMT2 increased lignin accumulation in Panax vietnamensis calli and Nicotiana benthamiana and improved fungal disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering lignin-mediated disease resistance in Panax vietnamensis or heterologous crops; can be combined with melatonin treatment or derepression of PvWRKY40
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PvCOMT2-overexpressing Nicotiana benthamiana showed thickened stems, compact xylem and increased biomass; no negative penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PvCOMT2 but not PvCOMT1 was directly repressed by PvWRKY40; MT alleviated PvWRKY40-mediated repression

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor with conserved WRKYGQK domain and C2H2 zinc finger motif
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses PvCOMT2 directly; suppresses PvCAD and PvCOMT1/2 expression upon overexpression, with strongest effect on PvCOMT2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator whose repression by melatonin derepresses PvCOMT2 and enables lignin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly and persistently downregulated by melatonin; reduced during infection and in MT-treated calli
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Suppressed in Panax vietnamensis leaves/calli after MT treatment and infection
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall reinforcement and fungal disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of lignin accumulation and disease resistance; overexpression lowers lignin and increases fungal susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; RT-qPCR; promoter cis-element analysis; subcellular localization; transient overexpression in calli and Nicotiana benthamiana; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; lignin quantification; pathogen assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate repression target for editing or silencing to enhance lignin-mediated resistance; useful with melatonin-based crop protection
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely influences growth-defence balance through lignin regulation, but no direct yield penalty quantified
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Neofusicoccum ribis infection; exogenous melatonin treatment; Fusarium oxysporum inoculation in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PvWRKY40 selectively suppressed PvCOMT2 but not PvCOMT1 in dual-luciferase assays; MT reversed PvWRKY40-mediated repression of PvCOMT2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvWRKY40 was the most melatonin-responsive WRKY and was strongly downregulated by MT. 2. PvWRKY40 is nuclear-localized and directly binds the W-box motif in the PvCOMT2 promoter. 3. Overexpression decreased lignin accumulation and increased fungal susceptibility, identifying PvWRKY40 as a negative regulator of lignin-mediated defence.
